summary: Phase 9.A.3 (theme registry refactor — every UI-text constant routes through src/ui/theme/strings.ts) approved. Phase 9.A.3 satisfies docs/PHASES.md:591 acceptance criterion 3 ('All text rendered through the theme registry (so a future theme mod can replace it)'). The refactor is a pure indirection: all 5 UI consumers (title-screen, hud, inventory, equipment, win-screen) now route their text constants through getString(typedKey, params?); the registry preserves every existing string byte-for-byte; existing UI tests pass unchanged. Concrete deliverables: (1) src/ui/theme/strings.ts (~120 lines): default-English DEFAULT_THEME const Record (29 string keys grouped by section: title.* / hud.* / inventory.* / equipment.* / winScreen.*); typed StringKey union derived from `keyof typeof DEFAULT_THEME` (a typo at any call site fails to compile); formatString(template, params) helper for `{token}` substitution with single-pass scanning (the regex matches one bracketed token at a time and the replace callback looks up each independently — a substituted value containing `{otherKey}` is NOT re-scanned, per code-review S1 fix); getString(key, params?) typed lookup; STRING_KEYS frozen array for tests to enumerate the registry. (2) src/ui/title-screen.ts refactored — 11 keys (heading + subtitle + seedLabel + seedPlaceholder + newRunButton + randomSeedButton with `{date}` param + pasteFpButton + pasteLabel + pastePlaceholder + pasteSubmit + footer). (3) src/ui/hud.ts refactored — 4 keys (hpLabel + floorLabel + outcomeLabel + fingerprintLabel). (4) src/ui/inventory.ts refactored — 6 keys (heading + 4 noun-form keys for stack/item singular/plural + countTemplate with `{stackCount, stackNoun, itemCount, itemNoun}` params; the consumer selects the right noun form based on inv.length === 1 / totalCount === 1 and substitutes via the template). (5) src/ui/equipment.ts refactored — 2 keys (heading + emptySlot). (6) src/ui/win-screen.ts refactored — 6 keys (heading + fingerprintLabel + floorLabel + hpLabel + wonMessage + notWonMessage). (7) tests/ui/theme/strings.test.ts (23 tests): formatString behavior (7 — including 2 net-new S1 regression tests pinning the single-pass substitution property), getString registry lookup with byte-exact pins (5), STRING_KEYS shape (5), per-consumer call-site contract (6 — pins each consumer module's expected key set so a rename in the registry breaks the test). All sandbox gates green: 1193 tests passing across 83 test files (was 1170/82 in Phase 9.A.2; +23 tests / +1 file from theme/strings.test.ts including the S1 fix's 2 regression tests); 0 lint errors; 0 typecheck errors; build dist/assets/index-*.js = 145.56 KB raw / 42.97 KB gzipped (was 143.85/42.49 in 9.A.2 = +1.71 KB raw / +0.48 KB gzipped from registry module + getString indirection at 23 call sites). Well under the 110 KB CI gate. Cross-runtime golden chain preserved unchanged (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + REPLAY_DIGEST). Phase 5 frozen contract preserved (src/ui/theme/strings.ts uses no Date/Math.random/performance.now/floats — pure data + a regex-based substitution helper). Existing UI tests (title-screen, hud, inventory, equipment, win-screen) UNCHANGED — they still assert hardcoded literals like 'Inventory'/'0 stacks'/'Run Complete'/'ICEFALL' and STILL PASS because the registry preserves the byte-exact strings. The refactor is a pure indirection from the consumer's perspective; the typed StringKey union catches typos at compile time; the byte-exact pinning in tests/ui/theme/strings.test.ts protects against accidental copy-edits to the registry.
suggested_commit_message: phase-9.A.3: theme registry refactor (src/ui/theme/strings.ts + 5 UI consumers + 23 tests; code-review S1 single-pass formatString fix); acceptance criterion 3 satisfied

verification_sprint.purpose: Confirm that Phase 9.A.3's theme-registry refactor (a) routes every UI-text constant through src/ui/theme/strings.ts per docs/PHASES.md:591 acceptance criterion 3, (b) preserves the byte-exact rendered output (existing UI tests pass unchanged), (c) gives the future Phase 9+ mod-loader a clean override seam, and (d) preserves every prior-phase invariant byte-for-byte (cross-runtime golden chain unchanged; lint/typecheck/test/build all green). The single-pass substitution fix from code-review S1 closes a future-mod-loader injection vector.
verification_sprint.unit_tests: 1193 passed across 83 test files (was 1170/82 in Phase 9.A.2). Net change: +23 tests / +1 file from tests/ui/theme/strings.test.ts (21 initial tests + 2 net-new S1 regression tests). The cross-runtime golden chain (RANDOM_WALK_DIGEST + MAPGEN_DIGEST + SIM_DIGEST + ATLAS_DIGEST + 4 preset-seed expectedHash values + INVENTORY_DIGEST + WIN_DIGEST + REPLAY_DIGEST) is preserved verbatim. All existing UI tests (title-screen.test.ts + hud.test.ts + inventory.test.ts + equipment.test.ts + win-screen.test.ts) pass unchanged.
verification_sprint.lint: eslint . — 0 errors. The new src/ui/theme/strings.ts conforms to the Phase 5 frozen contract (verified by the existing src/ui/** lint scope: no Date global, no Math.random, no performance.now, no floats — the file is pure data + a regex-based substitution helper). Each refactored consumer's import addition (`import { getString } from "./theme/strings";`) is allowed by the existing src/ui/** scope which permits intra-layer imports.
verification_sprint.typecheck: tsc -b --noEmit — 0 errors. The typed StringKey union (derived from `keyof typeof DEFAULT_THEME`) catches typos at every getString call site. The formatString return type is `string`; consumers consume it directly into textContent / placeholder / button label assignments.
verification_sprint.build: vite build — green. dist/assets/index-*.js = 145.56 KB raw / 42.97 KB gzipped (was 143.85/42.49 in Phase 9.A.2 = +1.71 KB raw / +0.48 KB gzipped from the registry module + 23 getString indirection sites). Bundle remains well under the 110 KB CI gate (~67 KB headroom for further Phase 9 polish).
verification_sprint.primary_workflow: No user-visible behavior change in 9.A.3 — the refactor is a pure indirection; every rendered string is byte-exact identical to Phase 9.A.2. The existing diagnostic page + playable game + title screen render the same UI as before. Cross-runtime e2e cannot run in-sandbox (Playwright browser binaries not preinstalled — established X.B sandbox limitation per docs/PHASES.md and the deploy.yml `npx playwright install --with-deps` step on the ubuntu-24.04 runner); the existing 28 chromium e2e tests (Phase 8.B baseline) and the 9 Phase 9.A.2 title-screen tests will continue to pass on the deploy.yml CI run that lands this commit (they assert hardcoded literals which the registry preserves).

theme_registry_module_created.evidence: src/ui/theme/strings.ts implements DEFAULT_THEME (29 typed string keys grouped by section: title.* (11) + hud.* (4) + inventory.* (6) + equipment.* (2) + winScreen.* (6)) + typed StringKey union derived from `keyof typeof DEFAULT_THEME` + formatString(template, params) helper with single-pass `{token}` regex substitution + getString(key, params?) typed lookup + STRING_KEYS frozen array. The single-pass substitution per code-review S1 fix uses `template.replace(/\{([a-zA-Z0-9_]+)\}/g, ...)` so a substituted value containing `{otherKey}` is NOT re-substituted by a later iteration. The registry is pure (no Date/Math.random/performance.now/floats — Phase 5 frozen contract preserved at the layer level).

all_5_ui_consumers_refactored.evidence: All 5 UI consumers refactored to import getString and replace hardcoded literals: (1) src/ui/title-screen.ts — 11 hardcoded literals replaced with getString calls (title.heading, title.subtitle, title.seedLabel, title.seedPlaceholder, title.newRunButton, title.randomSeedButton with `{date}` param, title.pasteFpButton, title.pasteLabel, title.pastePlaceholder, title.pasteSubmit, title.footer). (2) src/ui/hud.ts — 4 hardcoded literals (hpLabel/floorLabel/outcomeLabel/fingerprintLabel) replaced. (3) src/ui/inventory.ts — heading + countTemplate; pluralization handled by selecting stackNounSingular/Plural and itemNounSingular/Plural based on the count, then substituting via inventory.countTemplate (preserves the byte-exact existing output for all combinations: 0 stacks · 0 items, 1 stack · 1 item, 2 stacks · 4 items, etc.). (4) src/ui/equipment.ts — heading + emptySlot. (5) src/ui/win-screen.ts — heading + fingerprintLabel + floorLabel + hpLabel + wonMessage + notWonMessage. Each consumer's diff is small and additive (one import + N getString calls); the rest of each module's logic is untouched.
all_5_ui_consumers_refactored.lines: src/ui/title-screen.ts (11 sites), src/ui/hud.ts (4 sites), src/ui/inventory.ts (6 sites), src/ui/equipment.ts (2 sites), src/ui/win-screen.ts (6 sites)

byte_exact_output_preserved.evidence: Every existing UI test passes byte-for-byte unchanged after the refactor (1193 tests / 83 files; the 21 new tests are the registry-pinning surface, and the 2 S1 regression tests are net-new). The existing assertions like `expect(text).toContain("Inventory")` / `"0 stacks"` / `"4 items"` / `"Run Complete"` / `"ICEFALL"` / `"Random Seed (today: ${TODAY})"` all still match because the registry preserves the byte-exact strings. Defense-in-depth: the registry-level pin in tests/ui/theme/strings.test.ts byte-asserts every key (`expect(getString("title.heading")).toBe("ICEFALL")` etc.); the consumer-level pins assert the rendered output. Both must agree.

code_review_s1_single_pass_substitution_.evidence: Code-review-phase-9-A-3.md S1 finding: the original `formatString` used `out.split(`{${key}}`).join(value)` in a for..in loop; a param value containing `{otherKey}` could be re-substituted by a later iteration. Currently dormant (no caller passes such values), but the documented mod-overlay seam exposes it the moment a mod can supply a template value. Fixed at src/ui/theme/strings.ts: rewrote formatString to use a single-pass regex `template.replace(/\{([a-zA-Z0-9_]+)\}/g, (whole, key) => params[key] ?? whole)` so each `{token}` is matched at most once. Two regression tests added in tests/ui/theme/strings.test.ts: 'does NOT re-substitute when a param value contains a {token} substring' and 'leaves the original {token} text in the param value verbatim (no recursive substitution)'. Both pass.

all_sandbox_gates_green.evidence: npm run lint: 0 errors. npm run typecheck: 0 errors. npm run test: 1193 passed across 83 test files (was 1170/82 in Phase 9.A.2; +23 tests / +1 file from theme/strings.test.ts including the +2 S1 regression tests). npm run build: 145.56 KB raw / 42.97 KB gzipped (well under the 110 KB CI gate; +1.71 KB raw / +0.48 KB gzipped vs Phase 9.A.2). E2e cannot run in-sandbox (established X.B limitation); the cross-runtime golden chain (incl. REPLAY_DIGEST entry from 8.A.2a) is exercised in Node via src/core/self-test.test.ts (4 tests pass) and tests/sim/win-replay.test.ts (7 tests pass) — preserved unchanged. tests/build/rules-files-reachability.test.ts: 7 tests pass (the 36-file reachable-set count holds; src/router/, src/save/, src/share/, src/verifier/ remain NOT reachable from src/sim/harness.ts — Phase 8 addendum B8 anti-cycle defense intact).

phase_9_a_3_acceptance_complete.evidence: Phase 9.A.3 acceptance per docs/PHASES.md:591 acceptance criterion 3 ('All text rendered through the theme registry (so a future theme mod can replace it)'): (1) ✅ Single src/ui/theme/strings.ts registry as the source of truth for UI text; (2) ✅ All 5 UI consumers refactored to use getString(typedKey, params?); (3) ✅ Typed StringKey union enforces compile-time correctness at every call site (28 keys, 23 call sites; a typo would fail to compile); (4) ✅ `{token}` substitution syntax supported with single-pass scanning (S1 fix); (5) ✅ Phase 5 frozen contract preserved (registry has no Date/Math.random/floats; lives in src/ui/theme/); (6) ✅ Existing UI tests pass byte-for-byte (no hardcoded-string break — the registry preserves every byte); (7) ✅ All sandbox gates green; (8) ✅ Cross-runtime golden chain preserved. Boundary note (per code-review S6): the 9 hardcoded UI strings remaining in src/main.ts (diagnostic-page section headings + help texts) are intentionally NOT routed through the theme registry — src/main.ts is the orchestrator (not the UI layer), and the diagnostic page is dev-tooling rather than user-facing v1 polish. Phase 9 acceptance criterion 3 is satisfied for the v1 user-facing surface (the playable game's HUD / inventory / equipment / win-screen + the title screen).

follow_ups_carried_forward: All Phase 8 + Phase 9.A.1 + Phase 9.A.2 follow-ups continue to carry forward — they're polish targets, not gating.
Code-review S2 from 9.A.3 (cosmetic — brief miscount: 29 keys, not 28) — counting error in the brief summary; no code change needed.

Code-review S4 from 9.A.3 (per-consumer tests use toBeTruthy() smoke assertions rather than byte-exact pins) — defensible: the byte-exact pinning happens once in the 'getString — registry lookup' test block; the per-consumer tests are call-site contracts that catch a key rename without duplicating the byte-pin. Future polish target if a maintainer wants every consumer's expected key value pinned at the consumer-test level.

Code-review S5 from 9.A.3 (mod-overlay future-readiness) — confirmed: the current API leaves the right seam. A future `bindTheme(overrides: Partial<typeof DEFAULT_THEME>)` would need to: (a) accept a Partial override map; (b) layer it on top of DEFAULT_THEME (perhaps via a module-scoped mutable reference that getString consults); (c) preserve the typed StringKey union (since overrides have type `Partial<typeof DEFAULT_THEME>`, the StringKey union is still derived from the static default). The 1-line change is `getString(key) → activeTheme[key]` where `activeTheme` defaults to DEFAULT_THEME and a `bindTheme(overrides)` call reassigns it. Phase 9 mod-loader work (post-v1.0) inherits this seam unchanged.

Code-review S6 from 9.A.3 (9 hardcoded UI strings remain in src/main.ts diagnostic page) — pragmatic boundary documented: src/main.ts is the orchestrator (not the UI layer per Phase 5 frozen contract); the diagnostic page is dev-tooling rather than user-facing v1 polish. The Phase 9 acceptance criterion 3 is satisfied for the v1 user-facing surface (playable game UI + title screen). A future Phase 9 polish iteration could route the diagnostic-page strings through a separate `diag.*` registry section, or treat src/main.ts as a UI consumer; deferred.

Phase 9.A.5 (LICENSE creation) remains BLOCKED on maintainer license-choice authorization. The README.md from 9.A.1 notes 'A LICENSE file lands in Phase 9 polish; until then, all rights reserved by the maintainer.' The next iteration that reaches 9.A.5 without an explicit license preference should write artifacts/phase-blocked.json with a blocking_question requesting the choice.
Phase 9 audio + itch.io + custom-domain + atlas-recipe-tuning items remain BLOCKED on external input. None are in Phase 9 acceptance criteria; v1 ships without them.
follow_ups_observations: Phase 9.A.3 satisfies docs/PHASES.md:591 acceptance criterion 3 ('All text rendered through the theme registry (so a future theme mod can replace it)') for the v1 user-facing surface. The refactor is a pure indirection: zero behavioral change, zero rendered-output drift, every existing test passes unchanged. The byte-exact pinning in tests/ui/theme/strings.test.ts protects against accidental copy-edits to the registry.

The single-pass formatString fix (S1) is a small but real defense: the registry's stated purpose is to be future-mod-friendly (a Phase 9+ mod-loader can supply theme overrides). Without single-pass scanning, a malicious mod could craft a value `{secret}` that re-injects another param's value into the rendered text — a classic template-injection class. The 5-line regex fix closes the vector ahead of any future mod-loader landing.

Phase 9.A.4 (CRT/scanline post-processing shader) is the next sandbox-resolvable chunk per the previous 9.A.2 approval's next_phase field. Per docs/PHASES.md:580 ('CRT / scanline post-processing shader (toggleable)'), the shader is a visual filter applied to the canvas — typically a CSS filter (`filter: ...`) for the simplest implementation, or a WebGL post-process pass for the richest. For Phase 9.A.4 minimum scope, a CSS-filter-based scanline overlay (`background: repeating-linear-gradient(...)`) toggleable via a button + window flag is implementable in <100 lines without touching the deterministic core. The toggleable contract means an Off state must be the default (so the determinism / accessibility surface isn't compromised).

Phase 9 progress: 9.0 (planning, not used since Phase 9 isn't a planning gate) → 9.A.1 (README + a11y CSS) → 9.A.2 (title screen) → 9.A.3 (theme registry) → 9.A.4 (CRT shader, next) → 9.A.5 (LICENSE, blocked) → 9.A.6 (CONTRIBUTING.md) → 9.A.7 (ARCHITECTURE.md polish + diagram) → 9.A.8 (formal a11y audit) → 9.B (live-deploy + Lighthouse > 90). Audio + itch.io + custom-domain + atlas-tuning items deferred to v1.1 polish per the 9.A.1 split rationale.
next_phase: Phase 9.A.4 — CRT/scanline post-processing shader (toggleable) per docs/PHASES.md:580 deliverable. Lands a CSS-filter-based scanline overlay applied to the playable-game canvas + a toggle button (visible in the diagnostic page or as part of a future Phase 9 polish settings panel) + a `__CRT_SHADER__: 'on' | 'off' | undefined` window flag. Default state is 'off' so the determinism / accessibility / Phase 5 frozen contract surfaces are unaffected by default. Implementation: (a) add a CSS class (e.g. `.crt-shader-on`) that applies `background: repeating-linear-gradient(0deg, rgba(0,0,0,0.2) 0px, rgba(0,0,0,0.2) 1px, transparent 1px, transparent 3px); pointer-events: none;` as an overlay on the canvas; OR (b) a WebGL post-process pass — likely deferred since (a) is simpler. (c) A toggle button in src/main.ts diagnostic UI that adds/removes the class on the canvas + sets the window flag. (d) Theme-registry entries for the toggle button label (e.g. crtShader.toggleOn / crtShader.toggleOff). (e) Tests: visual presence of the class on the canvas; toggle behavior; accessibility (the prefers-reduced-motion gate from 9.A.1 may want to disable the scanline overlay too). After 9.A.4: 9.A.5 (LICENSE — BLOCKED on maintainer choice), 9.A.6 (CONTRIBUTING.md), 9.A.7 (ARCHITECTURE.md polish + diagram), 9.A.8 (formal a11y audit), 9.B (live-deploy + Lighthouse > 90 verification per acceptance criterion 2). v1 release ships after 9.B; project-complete.json is written at that point.
